tasks_completed.description: Change GRANT_TTL_MINUTES from 10 to 30 in scripts/write-commit-grant.py:38
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: 10-minute window shorter than observed dispatch-to-subagent latency (10-20+ min); matches bulk-commit sentinel TTL already at 30 min

tasks_completed.description: Update prose in commands/commit.md lines 86 and 277 to reflect 30-minute TTL
tasks_completed.changes: Line 86: timedelta(minutes=10) -> timedelta(minutes=30), '10 minutes from created_at' -> '30 minutes from created_at', '10-minute window' -> '30-minute window', 'on a 10-minute window' -> 'on a 30-minute window'; Line 277: '10 min window' -> '30 min window'
tasks_completed.rationale: Documentation consistency — prose references were stale after constant change

tasks_completed.changes: GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 -> GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30; 'was not changed' -> 'has since been updated to 30'
tasks_completed.rationale: Stale cross-reference note asserted old value; identified by BA codex review (pre-existing in BA context)

tasks_completed.description: Update test_final_sweep.sh:87 to match new TTL value
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex found this stale assertion — prior task added AC4 V4 to assert 'commit grant was not changed'; now that T2 intentionally changes it to 30, the assertion must track the new expected value

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: N/A — original design decision, not a regression
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: 10-minute TTL was initial estimate; subagent dispatch overhead and processing time were underestimated
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30 extends the validity window to cover the observed 10-20+ minute dispatch-to-commit latency; matches bulk-commit sentinel at 30 min

ac_verification.evidence: grep confirms GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30 at line 38; GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 not found anywhere in the file

dev.implementation_summary: Changed GRANT_TTL_MINUTES from 10 to 30 in scripts/write-commit-grant.py:38. Updated 4 prose references in commands/commit.md (lines 86 and 277) and 1 cross-reference in commands/push.md (lines 227-228). Codex found an additional stale assertion in hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:87 (AC4 V4 was checking for GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 as a regression guard from the prior task); updated to expect GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30. Total diff: 4 files, 6 lines changed in-place.

self_verification.build: skipped (no build system; Python syntax check N/A for pure constant change)
self_verification.notes: AC2 executed: script invoked with synthetic --task-id test123 --sid testsid --output-dir /tmp/ac2-test-grant; expires_at - created_at = 1800.0 seconds confirmed. test_final_sweep.sh AC4 V4 manually verified to produce PASS after the harness update.

dev.qa_notes: All 3 ACs verified. Codex-identified issue (test_final_sweep.sh:87 stale assertion) fixed in same cycle. QA should run hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh and confirm AC4 V4 PASS.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: One major finding: hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:87 checks for GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 as a regression guard from a prior task; after the T2 constant change to 30, this assertion would report AC4 V4 FAIL. PROPOSED_FIX: update line 87 to check for GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30. Applied.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Updated hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh:87 from GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 10 to GRANT_TTL_MINUTES = 30. No other findings — codex confirmed scope is appropriately SMALL (5+6 in-place line changes).

recommendations: Consider adding hooks/tests/test_final_sweep.sh to the list of files that must be updated when GRANT_TTL_MINUTES changes
